text:
Melody (センリツ, Senritsu) is a Music Hunter and a bodyguard for the Nostrade Family. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 8, Chapter 70 She is Prince Kacho Hui Guo Rou's Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 350 former bodyguard. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 400 

Appearance

Melody appears to be a short, young lady with androgynous features and a stocky build. Melody is balding and has straight silver colored hair that falls to her shoulders. Her front teeth are pronounced and protrude much like a rodent's. In public, Melody typically wears hats. Her clothes are simple and usually of earthy colors, though occasionally with pale pink accents. She always wears long sleeves and leggings, as the Sonata of Darkness has dramatically altered her appearance; her exposed flesh is warped and lurid enough to make Kurapika flinch. Her appearance before the Sonata changed her is unknown. Given that she asked whether Kurapika wanted to see a photo of her previous appearance and her longing to restore it, it is hinted that she was a beautiful young woman before the Sonata deformed her. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 8, Chapter 73 Furthermore, she takes offense of Kacho calling her an "old maid". As a Royal Bodyguard, she wears a black suit and tie. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 359 

Personality

Melody is a kind person and typically of a calm disposition. Due to her extraordinary hearing and natural astuteness, she is very perceptive and caring toward others' feelings. Her goal is to find and destroy the Sonata of Darkness so that no one else will have to suffer the fate that she and her friend have. She also wishes that she could return to her original appearance. She has a special affection for Kurapika and watches out for him, not swayed in the least by his determination to kill her should she reveal his secret. 

In the Black Whale, Melody displays excellent tactical skills to protect Kacho and her twin sister. Her emotional intelligence allows her to approach the volatile Kacho and win her trust. Moreover, she designs escape plans for the twins while also protecting them from various dangers, whether assassins or political threats. 

Melody is offended to be called old, as she glares at a smirking Kacho for making such remark. 

Background

Melody is a Music Hunter. Her life changed as the result of one night of drinking with a friend. She was deformed after listening to a single movement of the flute solo of the Sonata of Darkness. Her friend, who had played the piece, had his entire body severely mutated and died as a result. Subsequently, Melody gained supernatural hearing; she can hear things as quiet as heartbeats. Her goal is to find the sheet music for the Sonata and destroy it so that no one has to suffer the same fate. 

Plot

Yorknew City arc

Along with five other applicants, Melody applies for the bodyguard position in the Nostrade Family. The head bodyguard Dalzollene discusses the nature of the position, such as retrieving items for their boss. After the introduction, hooded assailants suddenly break into the room and attack Melody and the other applicants. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 8, Chapter 67 

After the applicants discover that Shachmono Tocino is behind the attack, Melody and the others look for the second infiltrator. She confirms that Squala is the one by simply listening to his heartbeat. The applicants take turns interrogating Squala by revealing their Nen abilities. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 8, Chapter 68 

Melody, Basho, Baise, and Kurapika, return to the mansion and deliver the specified items to Dalzollene (with Melody handing over skin from a patient with Dracoderma). Impressed by their work, Dalzollene starts to discuss the journey from the mansion to the Hotel Beitacle in Yorknew City. Melody and the others follow the head bodyguard as he introduces to them their boss, Neon Nostrade. 

During the first night of the Underground Auction, Dalzollene partners Melody and Kurapika together to keep watch on the Cemetery Building's front entrance. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 8, Chapter 72 During their surveillance, Melody asks for Kurapika's goal while she shares hers, explaining that she wants to destroy the Sonata of Darkness, which caused her drastic change in appearance and killed her friend. Furthermore, she admits that she wants to return to her original form and destroy all versions of the Sonata. 

Sometime after the auction begins, it's revealed that the auction items have been stolen. Dalzollene orders all the remaining bodyguards to pursue the thieves Hunter × Hunter - Volume 9, Chapter 74 until they eventually make their escape to the Gordeau Desert. Melody and the others witness the Phantom Troupe member Uvogin massacring dozens of Mafia members. Melody hears a faint heartbeat from underground, as the Shadow Beasts members Worm, Porcupine, Leech, and Rabid Dog arrive to confront Uvogin, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 9, Chapter 75 but all four are quickly defeated. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 9, Chapter 76 

Melody uses her ability to calm the enraged Kurapika and her anxious comrades. Although everyone is now in a calm state of mind, Kurapika still insists on capturing Uvogin and does so. They escape in two car, but Melody notices a needle attached to Uvogin's leg. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 9, Chapter 77 Due to the sudden interference of the remaining Shadow Beasts, the Nostrade bodyguards escape back to Yorknew safely. After she and the other guards torture Uvogin for information, the other Troupe members learn of their location and proceed to rescue their captured comrade. With the exception of Dalzollene, Melody and the other bodyguards manage to escape. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 9, Chapter 78 

Melody then contacts Kurapika and tries to contact Dalzollene, but to no avail. She suggests contacting Neon's father about the situation at hand. She nominates Kurapika to be the new leader of the bodyguards, which Kurapika accepts. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 9, Chapter 79 Later that night, Melody informs Kurapika that Light will arrive that night. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 10, Chapter 84 Upon Light's arrival, he orders Melody and Basho to escort Neon until she is safely back home. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 10, Chapter 89 Melody and Basho then accompany Neon and her attendants on a shopping spree at Lingon Airport, and subsequently lose sight of her. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 11, Chapter 94 She informs Kurapika of the situation immediately. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 11, Chapter 95 Kurapika later contacts her, and it's said that she, along with the other bodyguards, would regroup with him and Light. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 11, Chapter 96 

After the ensuing battle at the Cemetery Building, Light assigns Basho and Melody to take Neon on another shopping trip the following day, much to their chagrin, especially Basho's. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 11, Chapter 101 She informs Kurapika the next day that the hunt for the Troupe has been canceled after the Mafia found out that they are from Meteor City. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 105 When Kurapika decides to hunt down the rest of the Troupe, he contacts Melody, who later joins Killua Zoldyck in trailing the Spiders. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 109 

Melody tracks the Troupe's movement across the city and notes that Killua's steps are the most silent she has heard, which makes him reliable for Kurapika. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 110 They briefly lose track of the Troupe after exiting the station and update Kurapika about the situation. After Gon and Killua are taken hostage instead of Kurapika, Melody regroups with him and scolds him for his reckless behavior. She tries to get in contact with Squala, who has the Scarlet Eyes, but does not receive any reply from him. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 111 

After Nobunaga kills Squala, Pakunoda calls Chrollo and tells him that while Squala didn't know anything about Uvogin or the chain user's ability, they now know his name and appearance. After Chrollo tells her that they will be waiting in the lobby of the Hotel Beitacle, he informs Machi and Shizuku of the new development. A frustrated Gon says to himself that while he told Kurapika about carrying out the plan in a hotel lobby, they didn't decide on the time and he doesn't know where they're going. As Gon tries to figure out a way to let Kurapika know, Killua says to himself that Melody would have heard Chrollo's phone call, and Kurapika would know where they're going. He thinks that he has the perfect plan to blind the Troupe, but worries about the others figuring it out and not knowing when to execute it. He stresses that they need to be in complete synchronization and that he needs to find a way to communicate a time to them. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 112 

The two boys and the three Troupe members arrive at the hotel and wait in the lobby. Killua says to himself that the Troupe will be suspicious of anyone that enters the hotel now, hoping that the others are already inside. He then whispers to Melody to give them a signal, repeating it twice as Gon continues to think of what to do. Killua says that it's their only chance, but before he can whisper it to Melody, Leorio suddenly slams down a newspaper and yells at someone on the phone, making sure that they know the hotel's name is Beitacle. He sees them staring at him and asks if they want a piece of him. Shizuku whispers if they should deal with him, but Chrollo tells them to ignore him. 

Kurapika successfully captures Chrollo and escapes by car with Melody and Leorio. She tries to keep Kurapika from harming Chrollo, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 114 but nearly mentally breaks down upon hearing Chrollo's dark heartbeat. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 13, Chapter 116 At Lingon Airport, Melody confirms that Pakunoda is telling the truth about her identity and assures Kurapika that she can tell by his heartbeat whether Killua is being manipulated. During the negotiation with Pakunoda, Melody thinks about Kurapika's uncertainty. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 13, Chapter 117 

Later during the hostage exchange, Melody confirms that Killua is not being manipulated after he holds the phone to his heart. Along with Kurapika, Leorio, Gon, and Killua, Melody returns to the city after the exchange is successful. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 13, Chapter 118 

Greed Island arc

On September 6 th , Zepile asks Melody about Kurapika's fever as he lies asleep in bed with a towel on his forehead. Melody explains that her flute had no effect on him and deduces that his condition is not due to simple fatigue. Zepile suggests taking Kurapika to a hospital if he doesn't get better and offers to call a doctor he knows if visiting a hospital is too dangerous, which pleases Melody. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 13, Chapter 120 Once Kurapika wakes up, Melody tells him everything that has happened while he was out. As Melody and Kurapika prepare to leave the city from Lingon Airport, Melody promises Leorio that he will look after Kurapika, telling him that his heartbeat sounded the nicest in the city and that he would make a great teacher or doctor. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 13, Chapter 124 Melody briefly appears again telling Kurapika that Light wishes to speak with him. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 15, Chapter 149 

13 th Hunter Chairman Election arc

Melody votes in the first round of the 13th Hunter Chairman Election Hunter × Hunter - Volume 30, Chapter 320 and also visits Gon at the hospital. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 31, Chapter 324 As Leorio screams into his cellphone and demands to speak with Killua, Melody is distraught by Leorio's voice. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 31, Chapter 325 When Killua arrives at Gon's location with Alluka, Melody stands guard outside next to Goreinu. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 32, Chapter 331 She and the others all feel Nanika's tremendous power as it's used to heal Gon. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 32, Chapter 333 She then accompanies Gon and the others as Gon goes to meet Leorio in the auditorium where all the Hunters are voting for the new Chairman of the Hunter Association. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 32, Chapter 334 

Succession Contest arc

Melody is recruited by Kurapika to apply for one of the six listings for bodyguards listed on the Kakin website by the Kakin Princes, and is eventually contracted to be the bodyguard of 10 th Prince Kacho for the duration of her voyage on the Black Whale 1 to the Dark Continent. 

On the first day of the voyage, Melody heads to the ceremonial hall on Tier 1 of the Black Whale 1 to escort Kacho back to Room 1010. She overhears Kacho's plan to form an alliance with her sister 11 th Prince Fugetsu to take down the other Princes, spoken where Kacho believed no one could hear them, and forms an impression that Kacho is deceptive. However, Melody listens to Kacho's heartbeat as they walk back and learns that the bratty and petulant attitude Kacho puts up is a lie to herself. 

When Room 1014 is swarmed by Guardian Spirit Beasts, Kurapika contacts his companions to check on the status of their rooms. Melody reports that Room 1010 is clear of Nen beasts. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 360 The exact time is unclear, this could be before Melody escorts Kacho back to Room 1010, or after 

Four hours after departure, Kacho throws a tantrum when served a meal and knocks her meal off the table, berating her staff as she does so. Melody listens to the complaints whispered by Kacho's bodyguards and servants and worries that Kacho's spoiled act, if put on to protect herself, would backfire. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 366 

On the second day of the voyage, Melody and another Hunter stand guard as an investigator from the ship's Justice Bureau comes to question 6 th Queen Seiko after Kacho's servant Loberry is arrested for her involvment in Barrigen's death, and to place Room 1010 under surveillance for 72 hours. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 372 

At about 1:30 a.m. that night, during the early hours of the third day, Melody hears Kacho's heartbeat accelerate and rushes to her side. Despite Fugetsu being virtually beside Kacho, Melody does not react to the presence of a new heartbeat, indicating either a limitation to Melody's hearing abilities or that Fugetsu's heartbeat is not audible until Fugetsu steps out of the tunnel. On the way, Kacho is joined by a fellow Hunter, who also sensed something amiss. The two enter the master bedroom and Melody asks Kacho if she is alright. At the end of an exchange where Kacho stubbornly refuses Melody's gentle attempts at coaxing her to open up, Melody determines that Kacho should know the truth, and asks Kacho if she could talk to her about Nen. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 374 

Later on the third day, Kacho calls Melody over to help her with her studies. Kacho pretends to study while using a high-frequency device to communicate with Melody in Morse code, instructing her on the location of another device. Melody retrieves it and is moved by Kacho's efforts to reaffirm her resolve to save Kacho. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 376 

After Kacho convinces Seiko to ask Nasubi to allow Kacho and Fugetsu to perform at the Sunday banquet, Kacho updates Melody of their plan's progress, and that she would arrange for Melody and Keeney to stand by in a specific location. Melody acknowledges and reminds Kacho to ensure that her performance is played through all floors on Tier 1 through the speakers, and to remind Nasubi to keep the performance a secret to surprise everyone. Kacho meets with Fugetsu while Melody investigates the routes. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 377 

While watching Kacho go about her preparation, Melody quietly wishes for Kacho to stop playing the villain, guessing that Kacho's act was so that the guards would choose to save Fugetsu if they were forced to choose one of them to save, despite it not being what Fugetsu would want. Melody dismisses her thoughts, thinking that it would not matter if both could safely escape the ship. 

Keeney approaches Melody and tells her that he sensed a head appearing in Kacho's bed, as if coming out of a manhole. Based on Kacho's lack of response, they conclude it was likely Fugetsu using her Guardian Spirit Beast's ability. Keeney points out that a transportation ability used cleverly could thwart the succession war, but highlights that they would be the target of anyone who learns they lack a combat ability. Melody thinks that they have everything they need to organize an escape, but is worried about Kacho's Guardian Spirit Beast, which has yet to appear and might not want to withdraw from the contest. She tries to think of a way to make sure that Kacho will be unable to summon it. 

Sometime on Wednesday The exact time is unclear, it could be anytime from shortly after 10:00 a.m. Wednesday to shortly before 4:00 p.m. Thursday , Melody is called to the phone by a fellow bodyguard. Mizaistom has someone relay to her that Fugetsu was found on Tier 3 of the ship, confirming Melody's suspicions about Fugetsu's Guardian Spirit Beast's ability and is worried about information on it spreading. Mizaistom further relays a task for Melody to cooperate with Keeney to prevent Kacho and Fugetsu's escape, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 381 which contains a hidden true message, to do the opposite and ensure their escape. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 383 She relays Mizaistom's instructions to Keeney. 

Keeney shows Melody a picture of his late wife and daughter that he keeps in his pocket watch. He tells her that since their deaths two years earlier, he has been looking for a place to die and will commit suicide after Kacho and Fugetsu escape to make it seem like the Association was not involved. Distraught, Melody resolves to carry out the plan nonetheless. 

After Seiko and her investigator return from Room 1011, Kacho listens to music as Melody performs Hyle's Requiem for them, earning enthusiastic praise from Seiko who promise to arrange for her Sunday performance to be broadcast ship-wide. Melody exchanges a small nod with Kacho. Melody walks with the investigator to see him out, where the investigator expresses how moving her music was, giving her suggestions on how to help the two Princes and change the King's mind. However, his heartbeat sounds strange to Melody, too calm even for a professional, leading her to suspect that he is being manipulated or trying to thwart the twins' escape plan. 

On the eighth day of the voyage, Melody attends the banquet with Kacho and Fugetsu. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 382 She is scheduled to perform second to last, right after Keeney and right before the twins. She is relieved to hear that Princes Benjamin and Camilla, both Nen users, are absent. After Keeney's performance, Melody takes the stage, bows to King Nasubi, and begins to play, entrancing all listeners for three minutes. As she plays, she thinks about how Kacho's Guardian Spirit Beast never appeared, and sends them her wishes as Keeney leads the twins to the escape. Melody thinks of her conversation with Keeney, his words and heartbeat moving her enough to not prevent him from sacrificing himself so that the Hunter Association remains blameless in case the twins fail to escape. 

After Melody's performance finishes, Vantine informs Tserriednich about their entrancement. He explains that they heard a gunshot, but the moment they headed over, a beautiful landscape appeared in front of them. Vantine says that they were unable to move and guesses that it was due to the broadcast performance of a Hunter named Melody. Tserriednich asks him to call her over, as an "invitation" which Melody could refuse, so that they could make inferences based on her response. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 385 Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 387 

Melody is subsequently detained by the Justice Bureau, where she spends the night. The morning of the ninth day, Seiko's investigator comes to speak with her. He tells her that Keeney left a suicide note (claiming sole responsibility for the escape) which the Justice Bureau would likely use as an explanation, then brings up that Melody has received invitations from Princes impressed by her performance, to indirectly tell her of his suspicions that she was involved. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 386 

From his phrasing and actions, Melody understands that she is being held for her own protection, and that the Justice Bureau is a truly impartial branch. She asks about the two Princes, and the investigator answers that they are also in custody to be thoroughly questioned about how responsible they were for the escape attempt. Melody silently thanks him for keeping her and the Princes safely away from the other Princes, and prepares to use her time to consider her next moves. 

At the Justice Bureau's interrogation office the next day, "Kacho" (Kacho's Guardian Spirit Beast taking her form after her death) explains her theory that she was resurrected as a guardian spirit to protect Fugetsu, and that Fugetsu's ability to see her means that she is out of the contest. She speculates that Fugetsu's aura might be her fuel which would considerably burden her. "Kacho" partially blames herself for Fugetsu's weakened condition and wonders if Fugetsu finding out the truth will reduce the burden on her Nen. Melody listens to this and shakes and tears up as she blames herself for what happened. "Kacho" orders her to get over it and to feel bad when she's alone. 

Melody is reassured by "Kacho" that she did not regret escaping, having wanted to die and make Fugetsu king. "Kacho" says that since they can't escape, they should fight. She points out two situations to take advantage of — people thinking she is dead, and her later reappearance. "Kacho" adds to keep her death a secret from Fugetsu. The investigator suggests that "Kacho" should preemptively give Fugetsu an explanation for her presence despite the impression of the others that she is dead. He says that while he is still trying to come to grips with ghosts and Nen abilities, he was convinced by the military's reports and Melody's demeanor. Melody hears his calm heartbeat and doesn't believe him but keeps her disbelief to herself. The investigator prods Melody into agreeing to help him and "Kacho" by telling her that a girl half her age is waiting for her. 

After Melody's agreement, the investigator starts to update them on the situation and begins by sharing that five Princes have requested to see Melody: 3 rd Prince Zhang Lei and 4 th Prince Tserriednich made personal requests to invite her to their quarters. 1 st Prince Benjamin, 5 th Prince Tubeppa, and 7 th Prince Luzurus more explicitly offered pardons and requested encores. "Kacho" wonders what the pardon is for, and Melody signals and reminds him to keep quiet about Keeney's death. He explains that the Princes have a better understanding of Nen abilities now and have realized that Melody was involved in the escape attempt. Thus, they use the threat of a death sentence for her involvement to get Melody to perform. "Kacho" wonders what is wrong with Melody indirectly helping to reduce the number of Princes. Melody is taken aback by the harshness of her words. The investigator explains that since many higher-ranking Princes remain, many uncertainties remain, leading to the Princes restraining themselves. If they acted and performed while unaware of who is targeting whom, they could shoot themselves in the foot. Melody wonders if the Princes would give up if they knew that covering their ears would prevent themselves from blacking out. The investigator explains that their actions would come down to how Melody bargained, and speculates that they might auction Melody's "true encore" among the higher-ranking Princes. 

Melody is saddened to hear that her performance comes with assassination. The investigator says that the best way to protect Fugetsu is to deceive all the higher-ranking Princes and get rid of them themselves. Melody is shocked to hear him suggest killing Princes, and listens silently as he suggests a way to poison them and assures them that he can prepare everything if Melody agrees with the plan. Melody asks why he would be willing to do this for them and risk death if the plan fails. The investigator pauses for a second, but states plainly to Melody that he's in love with her, sharing that he's unable to sleep because he keeps thinking about her performance and is perplexed by the throbbing of his heart. "Kacho" is flustered, but Melody sits in disbelief, listening to his clock-like heartbeat. 

Melody agrees to help him as she tries to understand the difference between his words and his heartbeat. She considers that he could be trapping them but dismisses it since he suggested assassination, and instead considers that a Prince might have sent him to get rid of them. Melody is unsure again, when she realizes that he could have easily gotten rid of them and had no need to speak with them and suggest assassination. Melody then concludes that he must be a Manipulator, manipulating himself to appear as a non-Nen user to try and use her and "Kacho" for a hidden purpose. Melody returns to the idea that he is on a Prince's team, possible one that didn't apply for visitation, since simultaneously assassinating the higher-ranking Princes could be a real goal. 

The investigator says that he will get the supplies and asks Melody to use the visitations to convince as many Princes as possible to attend the next banquet. "Kacho" says that she will do her part and get Fugetsu to help by telling Fugetsu that the others think she is dead and getting her to understand that the top four Princes need to be eliminated. Melody watches "Kacho" leave through the walls, and is escorted out by the military 

"Kacho" returns to her room in the Justice Bureau and sees Fugetsu enter the room through her Guardian Spirit Beast's door in a drained, delirious state before Fugetsu passes out. "Kacho" contacts Melody and tells her about Fugetsu's state. Melody wants to see Fugetsu's state for herself, and has Kaiser adjust her meeting times with the Princes to align with his interview with Fugetsu, so that they can pass each other in the hallways. 

Melody walks by Fugetsu in the hallway and senses a dark presence emanate from her. Buzzing insects and multiple spirits are seen hovering around her. Melody is immensely worried by Fugetsu's state, and wonders how her condition deteriorated so quickly. Melody is directed to the visitation cell and has the visitation procedure explained to her by the guards. As she walks to the visitation cell, Melody realizes Fugetsu's life is in danger and decides she needs an exorcism. Melody also wonders why she can't see whatever is possessing her, despite the strength of the influence meaning that its source should be nearby. Based off the timing of the situation, Melody guesses that Fugetsu is a hostage that one of her visitors would use to negotiate. 

Melody sits and sees her first guest, Zhang Lei. She expresses surprise at seeing him in person. Zhang Lei tells her how much he wanted to show his respect and praises her performance at the banquet. He makes two requests, to hear her performance again and for her to accept a gift of his. The public defender asks if she will accept it, and Melody says that she will. Once the gift is put into the box and moved across, Melody sees that it's a coin with a "1" on it, which Zhang Lei says he made. 

Melody is then visited by Vantine, Tserriednich's representative, and picks up on his use of the word "negotiation". On Tserriednich's behalf, Vantine invites her to his quarters, but Melody cites her contract and explains she cannot decide on her own. Vantine changes his question and asks if she is 'unwilling' to play again. Melody says that she would gladly perform anywhere when asked and under the right circumstances. Vantine is happy to hear it and says that the Prince will be pleased. Melody then suggests that they could "negotiate" there at the visitation cell, but Vantine is confused, saying that they could work out the details after she is released. From their heartbeats, Melody rules out Zhang Lei and Tserriednich, and prepares to meet the remaining three visitors. 

After her visits, Melody meets with Kaiser and tells him to hold off on their plan until they identify who is behind Fugetsu's condition, and asks to be released on bail. Kaiser explains that easing her confinement means they won't be able to turn down the subpoenas from the Princes and shares that the 1 st Prince Benjamin's men are waiting for her to leave the Justice Bureau. Melody asks him to visit Kurapika in Room 1014 and to tell him everything since he's the only one she trusts, asking him to hurry. Melody wonders who the mastermind could be, considering that none of the people that visited her reacted to the word "negotiation". 

At 6 a.m. on the eleventh day, Melody is shown a picture of Fugetsu's back by "Kacho", with a prominent mark on her right shoulder blade. "Kacho" says that it looks like a bug bite, and it was not present before their escape, then Melody says it could also be an injection mark. "Kacho" dismisses the possibility, making Melody revise her guess to be a mark made by Nen. Melody explains that some Nen abilities involved a multistep process, and that Fugetsu had probably fallen into a trap and gotten marked by it. She asks "Kacho" if Fugetsu had said anything weird yesterday, drawing "Kacho"'s attention to Fugetsu excited about being able to use her ability more than once a day. Melody immediately asks "Kacho" to help Fugetsu realize the trap, hypothesizing that two doors would appear if Fugetsu used her ability, which would make her realize one is a trap. Melody thinks that Fugetsu might have been injected by a drug and says that Fugetsu is showing signs of addiction, since she could not think of any other reason for such a drastic change in a short time. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 402 

Melody tells "Kacho" that she was wrong in thinking that one of the Princes who had come to visit her was using Fugetsu to negotiate, and if Fugetsu continued using her ability, she would continue to weaken and would die before the next weekend, the result of an ability that indiscriminately attacks whoever gets trapped. Melody encourages "Kacho" to find out who is responsible and get them to stop, but points out that it would be difficult to identify them without any clues. 

The two continue their conversation later in Kaiser's office, joined by Kaiser. "Kacho" suggests that Luzurus' Guardian Spirit Beast was responsible, because Luzurus sold drugs to back his mafia family. Melody says that in the case that Luzurus' Guardian Spirit Beast was responsible, he would not be conscious that he was attacking, and so he could not be persuaded to stop. "Kacho" reasons out that the only way to stop it is to defeat Luzurus or his Guardian Spirit Beast, and wants to take action immediately. Melody tries to calm her down, telling her that they could not rush in blindly and needed a plan. "Kacho" says she has a plan, and explains that Fugetsu had been testing her ability and could travel to the inside of a lifeboat, but wanted to test whether others could enter the tunnel, and asks Melody for her help in testing that out. Melody pauses, then points out that they would need more people, someone strong enough to carry Luzurus while he was knocked out by her flute. Kaiser volunteers to be the person and asks them to do it while they are being questioned to establish their alibi, earning him a look from them. Melody says that it sounds like a fine plan, but asks where Fugetsu's door would connect. "Kacho" and Kaiser look at Melody, and after neither speak for a moment, "Kacho" says that it will need to connect to Luzurus' bedroom, then Melody says that Fugetsu would need to go there first. Kaiser does not understand, and so Melody explains that in order for Fugetsu to use her ability, she needs to have actually been to a place to revisit it with her Magical Worm. 

Kaiser thinks for a moment and says that he can deal with it too, and asks them to come up with a reason to visit each Prince's room, since maximizing the places they could visit would be best. "Kacho" suggests writing a "last message" from her to each Prince, and after thinking it through, Kaiser thinks it could work. Kaiser looks up and sees Melody and "Kacho" looking at him, and prompts them to say what they were thinking by saying that he thinks he had already told them how he felt about Melody. Melody waves his comment off and to make sure, she tells him that there was no turning back, and asks him if he was sure he wants to risk everything. Kaiser confirms he does, and as he explains his willingness to dirty his hands for a future with Fugetsu as king, Melody listens to his heartbeat to once again try and figure out his true intent. Melody does not detect any changes, is unable to figure out his true intent, and decides to assume he is on the enemy's side, and that he, or the person manipulating him, still considered them useful. She decides to maintain their current relationship in order to protect Fugetsu. 

Kaiser takes Fugetsu around Tier 1, and they make their way to visit the Princes. When they visit Luzurus, he unexpectedly hands the letter back to Kaiser after reading it himself, and asks Kaiser to read it. Kaiser pretends to do so, and thinks back to the conversation the three of them had earlier, where he suggested dropping information bombs using scandalous information the Justice Bureau had gathered on the Princes. After Kaiser suggests doing so, Melody asks how he planned to deal with the Princes wondering why Kacho would know the information contained in her "final letter". 

At 8:50 a.m. that day, the trio reconvene after Fugetsu makes her way around, and figure out their steps going forward. "Kacho" suggests taking action as soon as possible, but Melody points out that Basho giving Fugetsu a good luck charm to ward away the low-level spirits around her had bought them some time, and they had not yet proven a culprit. Melody proposes using her ocarina for logistical reasons, but says that she will not be able to do anything while she was playing. Kaiser says he can handle carrying Luzurus through a ten-meter tunnel. Melody thinks for a moment and brings up a rumbling that happened right above them and that Halkenburg was the only other Prince on Tier 2, suggesting that the ability that caused the rumbling belonged to either Halkenburg or his Guardian Spirit Beast. She points out that Halkenburg himself had fallen ill, and says that whether he intentionally fell ill or not was important. She asks to know the details, and for Halkenburg to be passed his letter when he awakens. Melody worriedly thinks about what to do next, thinking about how Halkenburg's letter contained all the information, about Benjamin's secret, Tserriednich's depravity, Halkenburg's own birth, and more information that would shake up the succession itself. She also hopes that Kurapika can make use of the information in his letter, to come up with something so that no more Princes would have to die. 

During their conversation, Melody points out that only 4 th and 13 th Princes had rejected their letter, and Halkenburg had been unable to accept it since he was under medical treatment. They conclude that their operation was a success, then Melody says that the hard part is next. Since Basho had helped her ward off the evil spirits even while Fugetsu was alone, Melody proposes letting Fugetsu sleep and get some rest, so that she can recover her strength. Kaiser suggests they get rest as well, and "Kacho" declares that they will carry out their plan the night of the next day. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 403 

In Room 1014, Kurapika prepares for the next round of Nen lessons, beginning on the next day, the twelfth day. Since 2 nd Prince Camilla had decided to send people to attend this round, Kurapika tries to figure out why, and notes that Melody's letter containing the secrets of each Prince did not mention anything about Camilla, and wonders if Camilla had a secret that forced her to act. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 404 

Equipment

Flute: Melody's main medium for her ability is a flute, similar to a Western concert flute. She uses it mostly to emit relaxing sounds to calm agitated people around her. 

Mosquitone Device: Melody owns a communication device which produces high-pitched sounds, similar to the buzzing of a mosquito, that only people below a certain age can hear. It is activated with the press of one's thumb and used to send coded messages (through Morse code, for example). 

Abilities & Powers

Melody has all the benefits granted by her status as a Hunter. She has considerable prowess as proven by the fact that she successfully retrieved an item as part of a test required to be hired as a Nostrade bodyguard. Contrary to her petite appearance, she is agile enough to fend off attacks from Tocino's Nen puppets, although her astonishment at Killua's acrobatic skills suggests that hers are not nearly as developed. She also knows Morse code. 

Advanced Strength: Melody was strong enough to flip a table with just one slap. 

Advanced Speed and Reflexes: Melody was fast enough to avoid hits from Tocino's puppets and to keep up with Kurapika while chasing the Phantom Troupe. 

Unfathomable Hearing: After listening to the Sonata of Darkness, Melody acquired extra-sensitive hearing and uses this ability in many useful ways. She can track targets or eavesdrop on conversations from hundreds of meters away. By listening to a person's heartbeat, she can discern their emotional state and whether they are lying or being manipulated. Melody can hear changes in recognizable heartbeats from a room away, but has not demonstrated the capability to detect one's emotional state from their heartbeat from anywhere farther than the distance between people in a normal conversation. She can even differentiate among hundreds of people's footsteps, enabling her to determine the number of people in a crowd while picking out the distinctive sound of her target's strides. Her hearing also enables her to ascertain the skills of others from the sounds they make, such as knowing that Killua is a former assassin from his silent steps. 

Masterful Musician: Melody is said to have mastered all manner of musical instruments, with the flute being the one that she can play best. It also serves as a medium to project her aura. Her skill with it left Queen Seiko astounded. Melody's musical talent also plays a part in allowing her to distinguish subtle changes in heartbeats and other sounds with her superhuman hearing. 

Morse Code Knowledge Melody communicated with Kacho secretly using Morse code while maintaining a fake conversation. 

Nen

Melody is an Emitter with dual affinity toward Manipulation Exhibition: Togashi Yoshihiro -Puzzle- who projects her aura when she plays her flute. Besides Hatsu, she is also able to use Zetsu.

Quotes

(To Kurapika) "I chose this job because I hoped one devil would know another." 
(To Kurapika) "His heartbeat is normal. He isn't upset or agitated. There's no dissonance caused by fallacy... no fear of death. It's not that he doesn't think he may die... This is the sound... of full acceptance of death...!! He enjoys the fact that death is always by his side... I've had enough! I don't want to hear it anymore! His heartbeat -- or yours!!" 
"Kurapika... You haven't realized the inconsistency in your own heartbeat...! No... the hint of dissonance is there, so you're aware of the conflict somewhere in the back of your mind...!! But you're refusing to acknowledge it..." 
"You see loathing when you face this woman. But she's here for the same reason you are... to get her friend back!" 
(To Leorio) "You know, you're very uplifting. You have the nicest heartbeat in this city. It's warm and gentle. I'd bet you'd make a great teacher or a doctor." 
(To Kacho) "I... feel pride in my work. I don't have anyone close to me I'd want to protect with my life like you do... but if protecting someone is my mission, I'm prepared to offer my life." 
"She's racking her brains in order to survive... I'll save her. I swear...!!" 
"After I heard his heartbeat... how could I stop him?" 

Trivia

Viz translated Melody's name literally: when written as "旋律", "senritsu" means "melody".
In the Crunchyroll translation of the 2011 anime adaptation, the music that Melody plays using her ability is entitled "Wild Flower". Hunter × Hunter - Episode 44 (2011) 
In the manga, one of Melody's casual outfits (e.g., in Chapters 109-111, 114, and 116-118) is similar to the uniform typically worn by Japanese kindergarten students.

Anime and Manga Differences

In the 1999 anime adaptation, Melody first meets Kurapika on a train rather than in the Nostrade's Mansion. Hunter × Hunter - Episode 45 (1999) 
In the same adaptation, it's shown that she can play the piano. Hunter × Hunter - Episode 48 (1999) 
Unlike in the manga where she brings a piece of Dracoderma skin in order to become a Nostrade bodyguard, in the 1999 anime adaptation she brings a piece of brain tissue from an assassinated dictator named General Sharman. Hunter × Hunter - Episode 50 (1999) 
In the 2011 anime adaptation, Melody first encounters Kurapika after he receives a job from the Sengi Guild. Hunter × Hunter - Episode 39 (2011) 

Intertextuality and References

In Volume 11, the author (Yoshihiro Togashi) revealed that Melody was inspired by the character Nausicaä and possibly Yupa from Hayao Miyazaki's manga Nausicaä of the Valley of the Wind, and Ashitaka from the film Princess Mononoke.
Melody's ability of listening to people's heartbeat is reminiscent to Shigeru Murota's tapping ability in YuYu Hakusho. The similarity is strengthened by Melody's reaction to Chrollo's dispassionate heartbeat before death, which parallels Murota's upon sensing Shinobu Sensui's thoughts, a character with whom Chrollo shares similarities.

Miscellaneous

In the second character popularity poll, Melody placed 20th with 64 votes.
In the third character popularity poll, Melody placed 30th with 45 votes.
